title: Slow-cooker macaroni cheese
subtitle: Macaroni cooked in a cheddar and mustard sauce
excerpt: Uncooked macaroni added raw to a simple cheese sauce and cooked on high for two to two-and-a-half hours. The pasta absorbs the sauce as it cooks, producing a thick, cohesive dish rather than pasta sitting in a separate pool of sauce. Finish under the grill for a golden top.
sourceNotes: Macaroni cheese has been made in Britain since the eighteenth century. The slow cooker version works because the pasta cooks in the sauce rather than in water, which means the starch released by the pasta thickens the sauce naturally. The result is more intensely flavoured than the stovetop version and requires only one pot.

content.text: Stir the pot at the one-hour mark to prevent the pasta sticking to the base and sides. The macaroni at the edges cooks faster than the centre, stirring equalises the temperature and ensures an even result. Without stirring, the outer pasta overcooks to a paste while the centre is still firm.

In a jug, whisk together the {{whole-milk}}, {{plain-flour}}, {{unsalted-butter}}, {{dijon-mustard}}, {{sea-salt-fine}}, {{black-pepper}}, and {{nutmeg-ground}} if using. Pour into the slow cooker. Add the uncooked {{pasta-dried}} and stir to submerge it in the liquid. Cook on high for 1 hour, then stir well, scraping any pasta from the sides and base. Cook for a further 1 to 1.5 hours until the pasta is just tender and the sauce has thickened. Switch off the slow cooker. Stir in the {{cheddar}} in two additions until fully melted and smooth. The sauce will look loose but will thicken as it sits. Scatter extra grated cheese over the top and place under a hot grill for 3 to 4 minutes until golden and bubbling.

items.fix: Switch the slow cooker off and allow the temperature to drop for 5 minutes before adding the cheese. Grated cheese added to a simmering liquid will split rather than melt smoothly
items.cause: Cheese added while the pot was still too hot
items.symptom: Sauce has seized into a grainy, lumpy texture

items.fix: Reduce total cook time to 1.5 hours next time. Macaroni in a slow cooker overcooks faster than in boiling water. Serve immediately once the cheese is stirred in
items.cause: Overcooked, or pasta left on keep-warm too long

items.fix: Add 50 to 100 ml of warm milk and stir before adding the cheese. The correct consistency should coat the pasta thickly but still flow from a spoon
items.cause: Pasta absorbed all the milk and released a lot of starch

content.text: Slow-cooker macaroni cheese is a weeknight shortcut that produces a more cohesive, saucy dish than the stovetop version. One pot, one grill pan, and very little effort. Use a strong, aged cheddar for flavour, mild cheddar produces a bland sauce however much you add.
